title: ADR-0002 re-implemented after partial workspace reset (2026-05-27, 25 new tests, suite 42 green)
content: On this run src/lib's ADR-0002 modules were ABSENT (reset to cn.ts+format.ts only), but ADR-0001's harness artifacts (format.test.ts, src/features/rankings/rank.ts+rank.test.ts) SURVIVED — so the reset was PARTIAL, not total. Re-implemented ADR-0002 from the settled decision: slug.ts (slugify ASCII-only), num.ts (clamp Inf/NaN-safe + RangeError, roundTo exponential-reparse FP-safe), str.ts (truncate ellipsis-budgeted, titleCase whitespace-preserving), colocated edge-case tests, no cross-imports. My three modules = 25 tests (8 slug + 10 num + 7 str). With ADR-0001 tests co-present the FULL suite = 42/42 green ~289ms Node v24.12.0, tsc clean. tsx was already in package.json this run; only had to add the `test` script. Lesson: resets can be partial — verify each ADR's files independently; suite total varies (24 ADR-0002-only / 42 with ADR-0001 present) so read the actual `# tests` line, don't assert a fixed number.

I verified the tree empirically and re-implemented from the settled ADR-0002 decision rather than trusting memory's \\\"implemented\\\" claim.\\n\\n## ADR-0002 (Context / Decision / Consequences)\\n**Context.** `src/lib` holds pure dependency-free helpers (`cn.ts`, `format.ts`). The three utilities are deliberately **file-disjoint**; the only shared surface is `package.json` test wiring (one-time, owned by ADR-0001).\\n\\n**Decision.** Each module is its own file + its own `*.test.ts`; **no module imports another**. `slugify` is intentionally ASCII-only. Tests use extensionless imports to satisfy strict `tsc`.\\n\\n**Consequences.** +25 tests for these modules, no new framework surface.
